channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Strip club Cinderella story 'Anora' wins best picture at 97th Academy Awards
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — "Anora,” a strip club Cinderella story without the fairy tale ending, was crowned best picture at the 97th Academy Awards on Sunday, handing Sean Baker’s gritty, Brooklyn-set screwball farce Hollywood’s top prize.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lethal injection, electrocution and now firing squads. A look at US execution methods
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — South Carolina is preparing this week to execute a man by firing squad, a capital punishment method that hasn’t been used in the U.S. in nearly 15 years.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Francis off mechanical ventilation entirely, signaling he's overcome risks from respiratory crisis
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in stable condition and didn’t need any mechanical ventilation Sunday, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Strip club Cinderella story 'Anora' wins best picture at the Oscars
item.description: "Anora,” a strip club Cinderella story without the fairy tale ending, was crowned best picture at the 97th Academy Awards on Sunday, handing Sean Baker’s gritty, Brooklyn-set screwball farce Hollywood’s top prize.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump's next first speech to Congress is bound to have little resemblance to his last first one
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nation will hear a new president sing a far different tune in his prime-time address before Congress on Tuesday night. Some Americans will lustily sing along. Others will plug their ears.
item.dc:creator: CALVIN WOODWARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Daryl Hannah voices support for Ukraine on Oscars stage
item.description: "Slava Ukraini!" the "Blade Runner" star said to applause as she stepped onto the Oscars stage to introduce the "Achievement in Film Editing" category.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Crews battle wildfires in North and South Carolina amid dry conditions and gusty winds
item.description: Crews battled wildfires in North and South Carolina on Sunday amid dry conditions and gusty winds as residents were forced to evacuate in some areas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tennessee could add 'covenant marriage' with proposed bill
item.description: One Tennessee lawmaker would like to add a new kind of marriage to the books through a bill filed in the General Assembly.
item.dc:creator: Erin McCullough, ABC 4

item.title: UK, France and Ukraine agree to work on ceasefire plan for Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: Britain, France and Ukraine have agreed to work on a ceasefire plan to present to the United States, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Sunday as European leaders assembled in London to discuss ending the war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Grocery shoppers are using these strategies to combat inflation: survey
item.description: For the vast majority of Americans who don't grow what they eat, it's not feasible to avoid eye-watering prices at the grocery store. However, a recent study found that most shoppers are getting creative to save money.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: How to watch
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — It's almost time to see how the biggest nailbiter Oscar season of recent years concludes. Stars will converge at the Dolby Theatre in Hollywood on Sunday for the 97th annual Academy Awards, which will undoubtedly see some first-time Oscar winners in top categories.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Republicans once maligned Medicaid. Now some see a program too big to touch
item.description: There's a mid-March deadline to avoid a partial government shutdown, and hesitation is surfacing among congressional Republicans about potential cuts to Medicaid.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Seitz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: GOP pushes ahead with citizenship voting bill. Some state election officials say it's problematic
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The centerpiece election legislation from congressional Republicans would require voters to prove their citizenship when registering, raising concerns among state election officials about how it would be implemented and who would pay for it.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Coast Guard intercepts 21 illegal immigrants off California coast
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard on Friday caught nearly two dozen illegal immigrants in a boat off the coast of Point Loma in California.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Woman arrested, charged after allegedly locking boyfriend in storage unit for 5 days
item.description: Investigators believe she asked her boyfriend to crawl toward the back of the "hoarder"-like storage unit to retrieve an item, then locked him inside.
item.dc:creator: Abby Mittower, KTLA 5

item.title: Lifelong New York civil rights advocate and NAACP leader Hazel Dukes dies at 92
item.description: Hazel Dukes, the president of the New York State chapter of the NAACP and lifelong civil rights advocate, died Saturday at the age of 92. Dukes peacefully passed away in her New York City home surrounded by family, her son, Ronald Dukes, said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Charli xcx’s' Brat reign continues, as she clears the decks at BRIT Awards
item.description: Charli xcx’s “Brat” reign continues, as she cleared the decks at this year’s BRIT Awards – the U.K.’s leading music awards show.
item.dc:creator: SIAN WATSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signs order designating English as the official language of the US
item.description: President Donald Trump signed on Saturday an executive order designating English as the official language of the United States.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Commanders acquire wide receiver Deebo Samuel in a trade with the 49ers, AP source says
item.description: The Washington Commanders have acquired wide receiver Deebo Samuel from the San Francisco 49ers for a fifth-round pick, pending a physical, according to a person with knowledge of the trade.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Zelensky won’t apologize for Oval Office spat, says it was bad for both sides
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky wouldn’t apologize for the meeting with President Trump, which erupted into a remarkable and tense spat, arguing that the situation was bad for both countries.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: Gene Hackman, wife tested negative for carbon monoxide, sheriff says
item.description: Actor Gene Hackman, his wife Betsy Arakawa, and their dog were found dead in their Santa Fe home, in a scene that one detective had deemed "suspicious enough in nature to require a thorough search and investigation.
item.dc:creator: Fallon Fischer, Fox 5

item.title: Pope had bronchial spasm, resulted in him inhaling vomit, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis suffered a bronchial spasm on Friday that resulted in him breathing in vomit, requiring non-invasive mechanical ventilation, the Vatican said in relaying a setback in his two-week long battle against double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Where things stand as Congress tries to avoid a partial government shutdown in two weeks
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As House members finished voting for the week and left Washington, the lead Democrat on the House Appropriations Committee, Rep.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Best mullet? Best use of Taylor Swift? AP hands out its own movie awards ahead of the Oscars
item.description: Hollywood's never-ending awards season can make it easy to get a little lost in the run-of-the-mill “best” categories. Actor. Actress. Director. Picture. A great movie can be reduced to a single performance; those that are left out seem to simply vanish for a while.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE and LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Washington man arrested for stealing $8M fishing boat with crew still on board, officials say
item.description: The suspect allegedly tried to steal a different boat earlier that morning, but its crew "ran him off the boat," police said.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, Fox 5

item.title: A stretch of a North Carolina highway that collapsed during Helene is about to reopen
item.description: A stretch of Interstate 40 through the western North Carolina mountains is reopening to traffic this weekend, months after Hurricane Helene's historic flooding collapsed portions of the road, partially restoring the major travel connection with eastern Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US lobster catch drops as crustaceans migrate to colder Canadian waters
item.description: SCARBOROUGH, Maine (AP) — The U.S. lobster industry's catch keeps sliding as fishermen contend with the northward migration of the valuable crustaceans. The industry is based mostly in Maine, where lobsters are both a cultural signifier and the backbone of the coastal economy.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gayle King, Katy Perry, Lauren Sánchez to board 'historic' Blue Origin space flight
item.description: "CBS Mornings" co-anchor Gayle King, singer Katy Perry and Lauren Sánchez will be among an all-woman crew blasting off on Jeff Bezos's Blue Origin as part of what's being called an "historic" space flight.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, WGN 9
